channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Global Foreign Aid Cuts Reach Record Levels, Australia Among Lower Contributors
item.description: A new OECD report reveals a dramatic fall in global foreign aid, dropping by $56.7 billion in 2025, marking the worst year on record for aid reductions. This steep decline, driven primarily by the Uni...

item.title: Durban's Wet 'n Wild Waterpark to close for scheduled maintenance from May 4 to May 26
item.description: Durban’s uShaka Marine World has announced that its Wet 'n Wild waterpark will be closed for scheduled maintenance from 4 May until 26 May. The eThekwini Municipality confirmed that the closure is par...

item.title: The growing anxiety over disrupted air travel
item.description: The article by Howard Chua-Eoan delves into a new kind of travel-related anxiety: not the fear of flying itself, but the fear of not being able to fly. Drawing on his own experiences, the writer notes...

item.title: Poems that comfort and guide us through difficult times
item.description: In times of uncertainty, poetry can serve as a powerful companion, helping us process grief, anxiety, and change. Five poetry experts share works they turn to for solace and reflection. Eugenio Montal...

item.title: Calgary Stampede canvas auction raises $6 million for chuckwagon drivers
item.description: The 2026 Calgary Stampede canvas auction reached an impressive total of $6 million on Thursday evening, showcasing the continued popularity and community support for the city’s most iconic annual even...

item.title: Bauchi Governor Reaffirms Commitment to PDP Amidst Defection Speculation
item.description: Governor Bala Mohammed of Bauchi State has firmly dismissed rumours circulating about a potential defection from the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) to the All Progressives Congress (APC). Addressing c...

item.title: FIA and Teams Hold Productive Talks on 2026 F1 Regulation Adjustments
item.description: Formula One’s governing body, the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA), has announced that its first technical meeting to review the new 2026 regulations resulted in 'constructive dialogue'...

item.title: FIFA Announces Full List of Match Officials for 2026 World Cup
item.description: FIFA don release the official list of referees wey go handle matches for the 2026 World Cup wey go happen for United States, Canada and Mexico between June 11 and July 19. The football body select 52 ...

item.title: Abejide Accuse David Mark and Aregbesola of Taking Over ADC Without Due Process
item.description: Honourable Leke Abejide, wey dey represent Yagba Federal Constituency for Kogi State under the African Democratic Congress (ADC), don talk say the party leadership under former Senate President David ...

item.title: Chico Slimani banned from driving for a year after drink-driving conviction
item.description: Former X Factor contestant Chico, whose real name is Yousseph Slimani, has been convicted of drink-driving at Willesden Magistrates’ Court. The 52-year-old singer was found guilty of driving a Vauxhal...

item.title: The Unexpected Benefits of 'Friction': Why Slowing Down in a Tech-Driven World Matters
item.description: Gaby Hinsliff's article explores a surprising sentiment: a growing concern about artificial intelligence and a newfound appreciation for the benefits of 'friction' in a world increasingly dominated by...

item.title: Kenya Court Approves Asahi Acquisition of EABL After Legal Objection Dismissed
item.description: The High Court in Nairobi has given the green light for Japan's Asahi Group Holdings to acquire a majority stake in East African Breweries PLC (EABL) from Diageo, dismissing a legal attempt to stop th...

item.title: Nitish Kumar sworn in as Rajya Sabha member, may continue as Bihar CM temporarily
item.description: In New Delhi, Janata Dal (United) leader Nitish Kumar on Friday took oath as a Rajya Sabha Member of Parliament, just months after beginning his 10th term as Bihar Chief Minister. His election to the ...

item.title: Biocontrol Programme Shows Success Against Water Hyacinth at Hartbeespoort Dam
item.description: The persistent water hyacinth infestation at the Hartbeespoort Dam, which has been a major environmental challenge since the 1960s, is finally showing signs of decline thanks to biological control met...

item.title: DepEd launches NSPC 2026 in Ormoc City with key reforms for campus journalism
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) is set to open the 2026 National Schools Press Conference (NSPC) from April 13 to 17 in Ormoc City, Leyte. Education Secretary Sonny Angara confirmed that all prepa...

item.title: US Travel Warning on Nigeria Amid Increased Security Risks
item.description: The US has issued a travel warning advising its citizens to reconsider travel to Nigeria, citing rising threats from terrorism, kidnappings, and civil unrest. States such as Borno, Yobe, and Adamawa h...

item.title: Asian Markets Edge Up as Investors Await U.S.-Iran Diplomatic Discussions
item.description: Asian stock markets traded slightly higher on Friday while oil prices held steady as investors awaited upcoming U.S.-Iran talks aimed at easing tensions in the Middle East. The restrained movement acr...

item.title: Iran’s Victory and the Shifting Global Order
item.description: In this article, Aasim Sajjad Akhtar analyses Iran’s recent victory against overwhelming odds, describing it as a defining moment in a long historical struggle against global imperialism. Despite ongo...

item.title: President Tinubu Visits Bayelsa to Commission Gas Turbine, Roads, and Bridges
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu dey Bayelsa State today to commission four major legacy projects, including one state-owned gas turbine for Opolo-Elebele. The Bayelsa State Government don declare today wo...

item.title: Tanzania and China’s Hongwang sign 33-year lease for Bagamoyo industrial park worth USD 3 billion
item.description: Tanzania has sealed a major investment deal worth three billion US dollars (approximately 7.8 trillion shillings) with HWTZ SEZ Limited, a subsidiary of China’s Hongwang Holding Group. The agreement, ...
